channel.title: 文献追踪系统
channel.description: 自动追踪 AI × 物理 / 化学 / 材料交叉领域最新文献

item.title: 兆量子位量子模拟的快速并行高码率STAR架构
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Refaat Ismail, Milan Kornjača, Hong-Ye Hu, Nishad Maskara, Sheng-Tao Wang, Hengyun Zhou, Chen Zhao
容错量子模拟需要优化编码开销、逻辑Clifford操作、魔术态制备和旋转合成。空间-时间高效模拟旋转（STAR）架构通过直接制备小角度旋转魔术态来降低其中两项成本。本文提出了一种高码率STAR架构，用于基于对称性驱动的算法、QEC码和中性原子硬件协同设计的局部晶格哈密顿量模拟。该架构利用目标晶格的平移对称性选择自行车链码，并允许在代码块中的所有逻辑量子位上并行执行STAR注入。

item.title: 单次执行中任意容损的量子位置验证
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Llorenç Escolà-Farràs, Boris Škorić, Florian Speelman
量子位置验证（QPV）旨在认证不可信证明者的空间位置，但面临纠缠攻击和光子损耗的挑战。本文通过利用基于无信号关联的不同技术，将基于承诺的修改推广到并行设置，从而在单次执行协议中实现了任意容损的QPV。该方法保持了底层QPV协议的安全保证，并证明了成功承诺的量子比特数k的阈值可以使对手接受概率呈指数衰减。

item.title: 马约拉纳-泡利稳定子码与费米子拓扑相的对偶网络
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Meng Sun, Zongyuan Wang, Nathanan Tantivasadakarn, Yu-An Chen
稳定子码为玻色子拓扑序提供了精确的晶格实现，但对内禀费米子拓扑相的系统稳定子描述仍不完善。本文引入了马约拉纳-泡利稳定子码，这是一类可精确求解的费米子晶格模型，其稳定子由广义泡利算符和马约拉纳算符构建。作为主要示例，我们构建了费米子环面码的精确稳定子实现，并展示了费米子环面码属于一个由任意子凝聚和规范玻色子或费米子宇称对称性产生的对偶网络。

item.title: 非局域量子相变
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Alessandro Coppo, Aanal Jayesh Shah, Hadiseh Alaeian, Valentina Brosco, Roberto Di Candia, Simone Felicetti
相变是微观对称性在热力学极限下自发破缺的典型例子，通常由热涨落或量子涨落驱动。本文引入非局域量子涨落作为驱动相变的新机制，表明环境模式之间共享的纠缠可以在远程系统之间诱导相关的对称性破缺，且与空间距离无关。研究发现，在临界点附近，环境中的量子关联主导了系统的临界行为。

item.title: 用于输出隐藏函数共享的量子原语
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Olivia R. Hartzell
本文引入了一种量子信息理论原语，用于确定一个依赖于多方局部私有输入的离散值函数。该原语允许各方相互学习彼此的局部输入并确定函数值，同时其各自系统保持独立于这些输入。由此产生的函数值在各方之间共享，但可以信息论上对任何外部观察者以及量子系统内的恶意状态制备或测量过程保持隐藏。

item.title: 量子条件互信息与信道容量
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 D. -S. Wang
信息度量通过编码定理获得操作意义，但量子条件互信息（QCMI）与信道编码的直接联系一直难以捉摸。本文提出了一种条件量子通信任务，并证明在第三系统辅助下，两方之间建立量子关联的最佳速率由QCMI的一半给出。这一结果将经典密钥生成容量推广到量子领域，并为可靠量子信息处理中的编码设计提供了新见解。

item.title: 基于贝叶斯优化和离散小波变换算法的快速鲁棒激光频率自动锁定
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Min Jiang, Xiao-Li Chen, Si-Bin Lu, Jia-Hao Fu, Zhan-Wei Yao, Shao-Kang Li, Min Ke, San-Ming Song, Run-Bing Li, Jin Wang, Ming-Sheng Zhan
快速鲁棒的激光频率自动锁定对于量子通信、量子计算和精密测量技术的现场部署至关重要。本文提出并演示了一种结合贝叶斯优化和离散双正交小波变换的自动锁定方案。该方法通过智能利用历史观测数据快速寻找参考，并利用小波变换识别跃迁信号以实现鲁棒的频率参考识别。实验结果表明，该方法在参考搜索速度上比传统扫描方法提高了五倍，并在严重噪声下实现了超过99.5%的识别精度。

item.title: 具有9公里中继器间距的高速率、资源高效全光量子中继器架构
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Ryosuke Shiina, Kenneth Goodenough, Nathan Arnold, Filip Rozpkedek
远距离量子通信是未来量子互联网的基石，但长距离生成纠缠贝尔对面临光子损耗和信号指数衰减的挑战。本文提出了一种全光量子中继器架构，通过结合连续变量和离散变量量子纠错码（GKP码和Steane码）来保护基本纠缠贝尔对，实现了1000公里通信距离和9公里等距中继器间距。该架构还引入了新的排名标准和基于多反射镜的光学腔作为自由空间光子存储模块。

item.title: 自由电子的量子层析成像
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Y. Fang, J. Kuttruff, Z. Zhao, L. Moehrle, P. Hommelhoff, P. Baum
确定量子系统的量子态是物理学中的基本任务，但自由电子的量子态测量和控制方法仍不完善。本文报告了一种测量连续变量中任意自由电子量子态的通用方法。该方法利用两束单色但光谱偏移的激光波产生干涉量子路径，直接揭示密度矩阵，从而揭示纯波包、系综及其相互关联的所有基本性质。

item.title: 使用非高斯资源进行连续变量量子隐形传态的相空间方法
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Ankita, Arpita Chatterjee
本文对使用光子减去双模压缩福克态（PS-TMSFS）作为纠缠资源的连续变量量子隐形传态进行了全面的相空间分析。研究了PS-TMSFS在Braunstein-Kimble隐形传态协议中的效用，并推导了光子减去过程的成功概率和相干态及压缩态输入的保真度。分析表明，隐形传态保真度强烈依赖于资源参数，并且对减去过程的变化高度敏感，但光子减去过程并未显著增强隐形传态保真度。

item.title: 电偶极矩和磁偶极矩的量子计量学：极限和最优方案
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Simone Cavazzoni, Paolo Bordone, Matteo G. A. Paris
量子系统中电偶极矩（EDM）和磁偶极矩（MDM）的表征对基础物理和量子传感至关重要。本文研究了在与电磁场耦合的通用两能级系统中，EDM和MDM单独及同时估计的最终精度极限。通过分析幺正、去极化动力学和热平衡态三种量子探测策略，推导了量子费舍尔信息，并确定了最大化估计精度的最优探测器和理想操作条件。研究还表明，正交偶极矩配置允许联合估计EDM和MDM，而平行配置则只能估计单个参数。

item.title: 二维双曲RNN神经量子态
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 H. L. Dao
本工作构建了首个二维（2D）双曲神经量子态（NQS），形式为洛伦兹2DRNN，并在2D横场伊辛模型中与欧几里得2DRNN进行了性能基准测试。研究发现，在物理学可由共形场论描述的相变点，双曲洛伦兹2DRNN NQS明显优于欧几里得2DRNN NQS。此外，本研究还将一维双曲NQS与欧几里得NQS在转换为一维设置的2DTFIM中进行了比较，进一步证实了一维双曲NQS的优越性。

item.title: 反常拓扑超辐射相
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 You-Qi Lu, Yu-Yu Zhang, Zi-Xiang Hu
本研究通过在光子Su-Schrieffer-Heeger (SSH) 配置中实现有限分量量子拉比阵列，展示了一组新颖的光-物质拓扑。我们揭示了物种依赖相的复杂光-物质耦合如何以不同于SSH模型的方式导致超辐射诱导带隙的闭合。研究发现了一种从正常相到拓扑超辐射电磁相的拓扑超辐射相变，其特征在于局部序参数和全局拓扑不变量。该设置提供了一个可调谐的拓扑量子光学平台，有望推动拓扑超辐射激光器的应用。

item.title: 量子计量学中抗噪声鲁棒最优测量研究
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Xinglei Yu, Xinzhi Zhao, Liangsheng Li, Stanisław Kurdziałek, Chengjie Zhang, Chuan-Feng Li, Guang-Can Guo
量子参数估计旨在实现超越经典方案的测量精度，但实际中噪声不可避免。本工作实验研究了基于费舍尔信息测量噪声敏感度（FI MENOS）理论的鲁棒最优测量。通过构建偏振马赫-曾德尔干涉仪，在受控噪声下实现了相位估计，结果表明不同测量对噪声表现出不同的敏感性。实验进一步证实FI MENOS代表了估计精度的最坏情况，有助于评估最优测量的抗噪声能力。

item.title: 受监控Rashba纳米线中持久的拓扑纠缠
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Emanuele Guida, Giulia Salatino, Gianluca Passarelli, Angelo Russomanno, Procolo Lucignano
本研究沿量子跳跃轨迹，探讨了受监控Rashba链的拓扑特性，特别是断开纠缠熵（DEE）初始拓扑值的持久性。结果发现，即使耗散作用于边界并影响拓扑马约拉纳模式，DEE仍能以与系统尺寸线性相关的时间保持其拓扑值。这一现象源于粒子不守恒和拓扑流形的简并性，使得监控可以在不同拓扑态之间切换，同时产生有限能量弹道传播准粒子的“中毒”，最终破坏拓扑纠缠结构。

item.title: 隐形斗篷中的量子可探测性
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Mohammad Mehdi Sadeghi
经典隐形斗篷旨在抑制散射信号，使物体对外部电磁探测器不可见，但这不意味着所有量子信息都被移除。本研究将经典隐形物体的可探测性表述为一个量子态可区分性问题。通过将线性无源斗篷视为作用于可访问探测模式的有效高斯量子通道，研究表明局部量子不可探测性要求探测到的第一和第二矩与隐藏物体参数无关。量子费舍尔信息提供了一个操作性判据，用于判断隐藏参数是否仍可从探测到的输出态中估计。

item.title: 纠缠光子的径向施密特模式探测器
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Radhika Prasad, Nilakshi Senapati, Abhinandan Bhattacharjee, Anand K Jha
自发参量下转换（SPDC）产生的高维空间纠缠双光子态是量子信息科学的重要资源，但径向模式纠缠因缺乏施密特分解形式和测量技术而未被充分利用。本工作首先从理论上证明，在典型实验条件下，SPDC双光子态的方位角平均可产生径向施密特分解形式。随后，研究展示了一种创新方法，通过表征密度矩阵来提取径向施密特模式及其谱。

item.title: 多模高斯玻色子系统中的Sp(2N, R)干涉测量用于最优计量和量子控制
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Chenwei Lv, Renbao Liu
高斯态玻色子的多模干涉仪在超越标准量子极限的量子计量学和玻色子量子计算中至关重要。本工作利用Sp(2N,R)对称性，研究了具有二次哈密顿量的多模玻色子系统中的量子计量学和量子控制。研究表明，最大化灵敏度的最优量子控制需要将挤压和位移对齐在同一方向。我们提出了Sp(2N,R)回波，作为SU(1,1)干涉测量的多模推广，以实现由量子费舍尔信息设定的相位估计灵敏度。

item.title: 固有退相干下多体二能级系统中相干参量放大的非线性动力学
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Muhammad Ibrahim
本工作研究了在包含参量放大、克尔型非线性和固有退相干的多体二能级原子系统与相干场相互作用中，全局量子失谐和量子费舍尔信息的动力学。结果显示，在没有固有退相干的情况下，两者均表现出快速振荡及明显的坍塌与复苏行为。强克尔非线性和强参量放大增强了全局量子失谐，而量子费舍尔信息在克尔非线性和放大强度的适当平衡下达到最大值。固有退相干会抑制振荡并使系统趋于稳态行为。

item.title: 在4000量子比特量子退火器上实现二维共振假真空衰变
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Gregor Humar, Jean-Yves Desaules, Luka Pavešić, Marko Ljubotina, Zlatko Papić, Kristel Michielsen, Jaka Vodeb
本研究利用一个拥有超过4000个量子比特的可编程量子退火器，实现了二维量子伊辛模型，其亚稳态自旋极化态编码了一个假真空。在特定纵向场值下，种子气泡边界处的单自旋翻转变得共振，从而实现了动力学受限的膨胀。结合实验、张量网络模拟和随机电路建模，我们观察到真空域的近弹道生长和亚弹道界面展宽，这与Kardar-Parisi-Zhang普适性一致。我们的结果确立了假真空衰变的生长主导机制。

item.title: 量子计量学中矩估计的有限次测量灵敏度
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Shaowei Du, Shuheng Liu, Weidong Li, Luca Pezzè, Augusto Smerzi, Qiongyi He
量子克拉默-劳界限只能渐近达到，并未指明具体估计器需要多少次测量才能接近它。本研究发展了一种针对矩量法估计的有限次测量理论，其中参数是从校准可观测量的样本均值而非完整似然中推断。对于一般的量子统计模型，其展开式用校准曲线和测量可观测量的中心矩表示。非线性校准曲线导致通常的矩估计器在有限次测量时存在偏差；我们构建了一个偏差校正估计器，其偏差为O(ν⁻³)。

item.title: 从谱结构到量子测温的传感极限
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Youssef Aiache, Simone Cavazzoni, Abderrahim El Allati, Paolo Bordone, Matteo G. A. Paris
量子温度计的精度受限于探针自身的谱结构，而能量级别配置与测温性能之间的系统映射为优化器件设计提供了重要信息。本工作通过分析广泛的量子系统，建立了这种映射关系。我们推导了量子费舍尔信息的精确标度律，揭示了两种不同的高温普适类别：有限谱探针表现出T⁻⁴衰减，而无界或连续谱则产生较慢的T⁻²衰减。在低温下，敏感度虽然普遍呈指数抑制，但可以通过设计简并激发态或全连接拓扑上的量子行走任意增强。

item.title: 张量网络表征与读出误差缓解
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yuchen Guo, Shuo Yang
读出误差是近期量子处理器提取可靠信息的主要瓶颈，尤其当空间关联不可忽略时。本研究提出了一种统一的张量网络框架，将读出过程建模为矩阵乘积算子（MPO），实现了超越非关联近似的高效表征和缓解。MPO模型通过对校准数据进行似然优化训练，并适用于非局部可观测估计、随机电路采样和随机测量协议等多种任务。在超导处理器上的实验和高达20量子比特的数值模拟表明，MPO模型能够捕获非关联模型遗漏的关联读出误差，且样本成本随系统尺寸呈近线性增长。

item.title: 从头算分子量子电动力学的多体二阶格林函数理论
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Amirhosein Amini, Jaime Cerda, Leopoldo Mejía, Arkajit Mandal
本工作开发了两种多体量子电动力学方法（CS-GF2和LF-GF2），用于计算强耦合光物质分子系统的基态能量。这些方法通过将电子-玻色子耦合纳入二阶多体格林函数理论（GF2）而构建，并分别采用相干态（CS）和朗-菲尔索夫（LF）变换真空态来处理玻色子部分。通过对多种分子系统（如H₂、LiH、酮-烯醇互变异构能垒等）的基准测试，两种方法均提供了高度准确的能量，其中LF-GF2略有改进。

item.title: 从少量副本中操作性检测任意量子态的维格纳负性
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Sudip Chakrabarty, Bivas Mallick, Ananda G. Maity, A. S. Majumdar
本研究开发了一个统一框架，利用少量状态副本中可实验获取的维格纳函数矩来检测任意量子态的维格纳负性。通过利用正相空间分布所满足的约束，导出了基于Lₚ范数不等式、对数凸性关系和汉克尔矩阵正定性的互补负性判据层次结构。该框架无需完整的相空间层析成像即可提供越来越强大的维格纳负性判据，并能对维格纳负性进行定量表征。此外，研究还建立了所有维格纳矩的精确多副本表示，作为基于奇偶性的可观测量的期望值，为其实验估计提供了一条实用且可扩展的途径。

item.title: 基于空间光调制器相位全息术的光子OAM量子比特通用量子门集模拟：单量子比特和多量子比特操作
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Saleha Maqsood, Muhammad Kamran, Tahir Malik
本论文对通过透射式空间光调制器（SLM）实现的光子轨道角动量（OAM）量子比特上的量子门操作进行了全面的模拟和硬件接地保真度分析。研究建立了一个包含8位量化噪声、扭曲向列（TN）电子和热噪声以及相位缠绕裁剪误差的真实三通道噪声模型，总噪声为σₜₒₜₐₗ = 92.4mrad。模拟了完整的通用单量子比特门集（X, Y, Z, S, T, H）和双量子比特纠缠门（CNOT, CZ, SWAP），预测门保真度在F = 0.9914–0.9936范围内。结果表明，叉形光栅门的保真度主要受TN噪声限制，而相位门由于零相位缠绕裁剪误差而实现更高的保真度，通过H-CNOT电路制备贝尔态的保真度达到F(Φ⁺⁾ = 0.9914。

item.title: 用于拓扑约束优化的霍奇谱代理
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Satoshi Kanno, Yoshi-aki Shimada
本研究提出了一种基于霍奇谱松弛和低通谱滤波器的可微分框架，用于拓扑约束优化。该框架从软图和软团复形构建了霍奇拉普拉斯型谱松弛，统一了图团复形和点云的Vietoris–Rips过滤。通过使用热滤波器、解析滤波器和多项式拉普拉斯矩定义了可微分的拓扑目标，并展示了其在点云上的应用。

item.title: 光子处理器中相位不稳定性的建模与分析
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Gökhan Elmas, Igor Litvin, Paul Kohl, Janis Nötzel
本研究旨在解决集成光子电路中可重构性和稳定输出信号的关键挑战，特别是光子处理器中由主动冷却系统和温度漂移引起的相位不稳定性。我们提出了理论模型来模拟光子处理器中的相位不稳定性，并与实验结果进行了验证。研究采用了布朗随机游走和基于实验观察到的振荡谐波的相位重建两种建模方法，并将其应用于光子处理器内部使用自反馈控制进行输入相位校正的特定应用。

item.title: 单腔玻色-爱因斯坦凝聚体的统一约瑟夫森动力学视角：从自囚禁到动力学相变
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Soi-Chan Lei
本研究探讨了与单光学腔强耦合的双组分玻色-爱因斯坦凝聚体（BEC），该系统可由补充了原子间非线性的平均场迪克模型有效描述。我们提出了一个统一的理论框架，证明了宏观量子自囚禁（MQST）在单腔内的两个内部原子能级之间自然产生。通过推导控制这种纯内部态结构的无量纲半经典约瑟夫森方程（SJE），我们解析地确定了相变的临界非线性阈值和内在相移机制。基于此框架，我们提出了两种操纵量子相变的方法：通过光子泵浦进行动态原位调谐，以及通过实时参数骤变诱导非平衡动力学相变（DPT）。

item.title: 从瓦砾模拟到主动磁力测绘：量子传感在灾害响应中的应用
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Samuel Tovey, Stefan Prestel, Hiroshi Yamauchi
本论文提出将基于无人机的量子磁力测量作为一种补充模式，用于在灾害响应中定位建筑物倒塌后的幸存者。研究开发了一个涵盖瓦砾物理、传感器阵列部署和主动空间重建的模拟流程，使用虚幻引擎生成钢筋混凝土停车场倒塌场景并计算感应磁场。结果表明，从屋顶上方约1米处可恢复亚皮特斯拉至亚纳特斯拉范围内的有意义磁结构，且三传感器阵列的主动采样在大约100个样本内达到峰值结构相关性，这表明量子级传感器在灾害响应中的巨大潜力。

item.title: 晶体绝缘体中的高阶贝里曲率、第二陈数和磁电耦合
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Niclas Heinsdorf, Ken Shiozaki
本研究将四维陈绝缘体的晶格模型重写为三维布里渊区上的平移不变无限链族，并使用无限矩阵乘积态（iMPS）计算其高阶三形式贝里曲率。我们计算了相关Dixmier–Douady–Kapustin–Spodyneiko（DDKS）数作为模型质量项函数的拓扑相图，并证明其与第二陈数的相图完全一致。这一结果表明，高阶贝里曲率可以以明显量子化的方式计算第二陈数。受第二陈形式与陈-西蒙斯轴子耦合之间联系的启发，我们研究了三维中的磁电耦合及其与高阶贝里相位的关系。

item.title: 利用循环光学跃迁对量子点自旋进行相干控制
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Zhe Xian Koong, Urs Haeusler, Jan M. Kaspari, Christian Schimpf, Benyam Dejen, Ahmed M. Hassanen, Daniel Graham, Yusuf Karli, Ailton J. Garcia Jr., Melina Peter, Edmund Clarke, Maxime Hugues, Michał Gawełczyk, Armando Rastelli, Doris E. Reiter, Mete Atatüre, Dorian A. Gangloff
固态自旋有望成为连接固定量子比特和单光子的量子通信技术接口。本研究通过利用轻空穴混合，在法拉第构型中带负电荷的重空穴激子中实现了高度不对称的Lambda系统。通过补偿GHz量级的微分斯塔克位移并进行核自旋冷却，我们实现了电子自旋量子比特的量子控制，π脉冲对比度达到97.4%，同时保持了471（50）的自旋选择性光学跃迁循环性。该方案在GaAs和InGaAs量子点中均得到验证，并与核量子存储器的操作兼容。

item.title: 超单态的蒸馏
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Saeed Ahmad, Shuang Li, Jonathan Raghoonanan, Kaixuan Zhou, Valentin Ivannikov, Tim Byrnes
本研究提出了一种针对由N个量子比特组成的超单态的纠缠蒸馏（纯化）协议。超单态是一种总自旋为零且自旋方差为零的态，具有涉及所有量子比特的完全纠缠结构。在我们的蒸馏协议中，通过在局部总自旋基下测量初始自旋为零的三个副本，并在后选择后生成更高保真度的超单态。该协议仅使用局部操作和经典通信，适用于量子时钟同步和密码学等远距离应用，并避免了高维舒尔变换，可用于量子计量学等任务。

item.title: 预示单光子光谱亮度的基本极限
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Tse-Yu Lin, Wei-Kai Huang, Pei-Yu Tu, Yong-Fan Chen, Ite A. Yu
预示单光子（HSPs）因其消除到达时间随机性并提高传输可靠性的能力，成为量子通信和网络中多功能的飞行量子比特。本研究系统地探讨了HSPs的光谱亮度（SB，定义为每单位线宽的生成速率）及其与交叉相关函数（或信噪比SBR）的关系。我们最终推导出了适用于所有HSP源的SB上限，该上限由一个新定义的品质因数（SBR与有效SB的乘积）决定。通过使用基于热原子蒸气的HSP源，在单光子标准下，我们实现了(8.5±0.3)×10⁵对/秒/MHz的SB和0.73±0.02的品质因数，这两个值均代表了当前记录。

item.title: 超越Trotter化：用于量子模拟的变分乘积公式
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Ibsal Assi, Michael Vogl, Meenu Kumari, J. P. F. LeBlanc
本研究提出了一种替代Trotter-Suzuki分解的变分方法，用于量子模拟，该方法在保持时间演化酉结构的同时，提供了对误差的更大控制。我们提出的变分参数源自全局作用原理，其欧拉-拉格朗日方程控制着它们的最佳动力学。与传统的基于波函数的变分方法不同，我们的方法专门针对时间演化操作，这使得一组优化的参数可以应用于固定哈密顿量的任何初始状态，避免了昂贵的优化过程。我们的方法优于标准的Trotter-Suzuki公式，通常比高阶Suzuki方案实现更高的精度，从而在量子计算应用中设计出更少门数的量子电路，减少噪声并提高精度。

item.title: 拓扑量子场论和广义统计的泡利稳定器形式
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yitao Feng, Hanyu Xue, Ryohei Kobayashi, Po-Shen Hsin, Yu-An Chen
本文构建了作为泡利稳定器模型的新型格点规范理论，实现了各种维度下的拓扑量子场论（TQFTs）。研究人员开发了扩展激发态的格点描述，并系统地确定了它们的广义统计特性。主要例子是(4+1)维费米子环面码，其环激发表现出费米子环统计。这些模型实现了(4+1)维中所有扭曲的2-形式规范理论和高阶Dijkgraaf-Witten TQFT。

item.title: 可积和基态可解模型的分类：图上的Jastrow波函数和母哈密顿量
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Nilanjan Sasmal, Adolfo del Campo
本文引入了一类可区分连续变量多体系统，其粒子间相互作用由图的邻接矩阵决定。这些系统的基态波函数呈现广义Jastrow形式，涉及图边集上的对关联函数乘积。研究表明，相应的母哈密顿量包含由图邻接矩阵决定的两体相互作用以及图上所有可能2-路径上的三体相互作用。该工作利用图论元素，绘制了模型图景，并提供了大量新的基态可解模型实例。

item.title: 通过表面码量子纠错实现非局域拓扑麦克斯韦妖的功能传输
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 M. Y. Abd-Rabbou, Cong-Feng Qiao
本文提出了一种非局域麦克斯韦妖协议，利用共享的表面码，通过局域操作和经典通信在空间分离的量子电池之间传输功能。该协议通过主动综合征监测，将逻辑错误率指数级抑制到拓扑阈值以下，从而将物理量子比特直接转换为可恢复的功能。研究发现，对于有限尺寸的编码，净提取功在热力学临界错误率处改变符号，揭示了一个重要的有限尺寸效应。

item.title: 近乎对易哈密顿量的舍入
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Islam Faisal, Anand Natarajan, Alexander Poremba
本文展示了如何有效地将任意近乎对易的2-局域量子比特哈密顿量近似为一个对易哈密顿量。研究人员提出了一种新的保持局域性的算法舍入技术，将具有近似对易项的哈密顿量映射到一个项之间两两对易的邻近哈密顿量。因此，对于近似对易的2-局域量子比特哈密顿量，其基态能量的近似值在特定条件下属于NP复杂度类。该舍入框架还应用于吉布斯采样和快速哈密顿量模拟。

item.title: 量子启发式地形立体视觉
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Fanglin Bao, Youfei Xie, Syed Masood
本文重新审视了远距离立体视觉中的传统三角测量，并指出绝对距离和相对地形的最佳测量方法存在不兼容性。为解决此问题，研究人员引入了立体正则化，并提出了一种地形干涉仪，该干涉仪利用交叉探测器相关性来探测地形，而无需测量距离剖面。这种Fizeau成像干涉仪通过平衡零差检测，饱和了量子费舍尔信息，并改善了地形误差标度，从而实现了超越瑞利极限的热源地形立体视觉。

item.title: 量子传感器波状暗物质搜索中类量子纠错的噪声抑制
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Hajime Fukuda, Takeo Moroi, Thanaporn Sichanugrist
本文提出了一种类量子纠错的噪声抑制协议，旨在提高量子传感器搜索波状暗物质的灵敏度。该协议利用多个传感器来减轻单个传感器的噪声，从而抑制与暗物质信号平行的激发噪声。研究表明，该协议能将暗物质信号的灵敏度提高√N倍，并且对于足够大的N，其性能可达到理想测量的标准量子极限。该工作有望广泛应用于具有未知相位的各类信号，并增强共振腔阵列等量子传感器的灵敏度。

item.title: 非平衡对称破缺相变中的不对称动力学
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Liv Hammer, Colin Rylands, Federico Carollo
本文分析了开放量子多体系统中不对称性的动力学，该系统具有对称破缺特性，并揭示了该环境下独特的动力学行为。在对称相中，研究人员证明了量子Mpemba效应的存在，这是不对称性非单调演化的直接结果。在对称破缺相中，本文分析了系统增加或减少不对称性能力之间的不平衡。这些结果将量子Mpemba效应的概念扩展到展现对称破缺相变的开放量子多体系统。

item.title: 基于量子反点干涉仪的非阿贝尔任意子编织
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Junyu Tang, Gang v. Chen
本文提出了一种利用两个门控量子反点探测非阿贝尔任意子基本交换的干涉仪。通过独立调节两个反点，该装置实现了不同的协同隧穿过程，对应于非阿贝尔任意子的不同编织。对于未分辨的局域融合通道，单次和双次协同过程的干涉信号差异可用于测量非阿贝尔任意子的基本交换，从而直接探测其非阿贝尔统计和拓扑自旋。这项工作为操纵和探测具有基本分数统计的非阿贝尔编织提供了一条有前景且实用的途径。

item.title: 束缚在电荷陷阱中的任意子的局域光谱学
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Jeong Min Park, Cristian Voinea, Yen-Chen Tsui, Songyang Pu, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Nigel R. Cooper, Michael P. Zaletel, Zlatko Papić, Ali Yazdani
本文利用扫描隧道显微镜/光谱学（STM/STS）研究了单层石墨烯中整数和分数量子霍尔态在单个带电杂质附近的激发谱。在分数量子霍尔态中，研究人员观察到最低能量光谱特征的额外能级分裂，这仅在化学势位于分数能隙内时出现。这一现象归因于被杂质势阱捕获的任意子的多体构型。数值计算结果进一步支持了这一解释。

item.title: 拓扑平带中由磁平移对称性产生的精确可解对密度波
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Zhengzhi Wu, Zhou-Quan Wan, Steven H. Simon
本文利用广义量子几何嵌套（QGN）框架，构建了在拓扑平带中具有对密度波（PDW）基态的精确可解模型。该构造广泛适用于具有非对易磁平移对称性（MTS）和时间反演对称性的系统。例如，具有相反陈数的两个理想平坦陈带的Kapit-Mueller模型的时间反演不变版本。这项工作为进一步研究PDW超导体中的能带拓扑和量子几何提供了平台。

item.title: 通过MoSe₂/WS₂异质结构中的莫尔激子探测层可调哈伯德带
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Hongyu Yao, Qiao Li, Chih-En Hsu, Takashi Taniguchi, Kenji Watanabe, Hung-Chung Hsueh, Zhenglu Li, Andrew Y. Joe
本文通过对双栅、60度对齐的MoSe₂/WS₂异质双层进行光学光谱分析，报告了电场驱动的层特定哈伯德带重排。研究人员利用两个空间上不同的莫尔激子作为局部光学探针，定量提取了层依赖的局域库仑排斥能。此外，通过静电调谐系统至II型能带对齐，研究人员稳定了广义维格纳晶体和条纹相。这些结果确立了垂直电场作为层选择性哈伯德物理的确定性调谐旋钮。

item.title: 拓扑相变中的科恩反常
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Jingwen Li, Arpita Dutta, Kush Saha, Andrzej Szczerbakow, Tomasz Story, Manfred Fiebig, Shovon Pal
本文通过温度依赖的时域太赫兹光谱，揭示了Pb0.77Sn0.23Se在拓扑相变过程中低能表面声子模式的显著软化。与铁电体中声子软化不同，这种效应并非结构不稳定性，而是反映了电子重构。研究人员将这种软化归因于科恩反常，表明拓扑相中晶格振动与狄拉克类表面电子之间存在强耦合。声子线宽偏离了标准的非谐温度依赖性，进一步证实了这一发现。

item.title: 基于非对易传输矩阵的格点不可逆对称性
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Eric Vernier, Yuan Miao, Masahito Yamazaki
本文建立了XXZ自旋链在单位根处的Onsager对称性、对偶缺陷与量子可积性之间的直接联系。通过使用由非平衡Yang-Baxter/RLL关系控制的非阿贝尔传输矩阵代数，作者构建了Onsager代数及其对偶自同构的显式格点实现。该对偶性由与τ₂模型传输矩阵相关的矩阵乘积算符表示，其遵循Z_N Tambara-Yamagami融合规则，从而在格点上实现了自由紧致玻色子共形场论的拓扑缺陷线。研究结果表明，非阿贝尔可积性是格点模型中Onsager对称性和范畴对偶性出现的自然框架。

item.title: 两体相互作用对Floquet拓扑相的影响
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Arijit Dutta, Souradeep Roy Choudhury, Tao Qin, Walter Hofstetter
本文利用实空间Floquet动力学平均场理论（DMFT）研究了蜂窝晶格上圆周驱动的Falicov-Kimball模型。该模型的非相互作用版本在实验中已实现，并展现出Haldane相和异常拓扑相。研究探讨了两体相互作用U对这些相稳定性的影响，发现随着U的增加，电荷泵浦不再量子化，这归因于边缘模式的展宽。此外，研究还计算了能量耗散率，发现在两种区域中表现出显著不同的行为。

item.title: 一种结合紧束缚与机器学习势能模型的镁化合物模型
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Jiwen Yu, Arash A. Mostofi, Andrew Horsfield
本文提出了一种用于镁基系统的模型，该模型结合了密度泛函紧束缚（DFTB）与MACE机器学习原子间势（DFTB+MACE）。在该模型中，传统的排斥势（对势）被多体MACE势取代。研究人员利用该模型进行了MgO-CO2吸附系统的结构弛豫、水团簇的分子动力学计算以及稳定fcc-MgO和亚稳态bcc-MgO结构的声子谱计算。结果表明，DFTB+MACE模型相对于带有对势的DFTB模型提高了准确性，且计算成本仅适度增加，同时还能提供大多数机器学习势无法提供的电子结构信息。

item.title: 具有低精细结构分裂的电信波段位点控制量子点
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Christian C. Ruiz Madera, Paweł Holewa, Paweł Wyborski, Meng Xiong, Battulga Munkhbat, Elizaveta Semenova
本文展示了在截锥形InP纳米金字塔对称顶点处进行InAs/InP量子点（QD）的位点控制Stranski-Krastanov（SK）生长。这种方法抑制了各向异性生长，从而形成了高度对称的量子点，其精细结构分裂（FSS）低于9.2 μeV的统计验证分辨率极限。通过光刻定义的成核位点，实现了对量子点位置的确定性控制，克服了传统SK生长的局限性。单个对称量子点的高结构质量通过其在S、C和L电信波段的单光子发射（g⁽²⁾(0)=0.07）得到证实，且未发现光刻诱导的缺陷影响。

item.title: 量子和经典系统中典型性的异同
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Peter Reimann, Nicolas Nessi
典型性是量子多体系统的一个普遍且非常一般的性质，指在足够高维的希尔伯特（子）空间中，绝大多数纯态（归一化矢量）的任何给定可观测量的期望值实际上是无法区分的。本文提供了非常简单和普遍的论证，表明经典多体系统中的纯态（相空间点）的类似典型性对于宏观可观测物仍然成立，但对于微观（少体）可观测物则不再成立。

item.title: 二维分数量子临界点处的涌现时空超对称性
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Zhengzhi Wu, Zhou-Quan Wan, Shao-Kai Jian, Hong Yao
尽管粒子物理学中时空超对称性（SUSY）的实验证据仍然难以捉摸，但凝聚态系统为量子临界点（QCPs）处超对称性的涌现提供了一个有前景的平台。本文研究了Kitaev蜂窝模型中具有Su-Schrieffer-Heeger（SSH）自旋-声子耦合的分数量子临界点处涌现的N=2时空超对称性。通过数值计算和分析，研究表明各向异性SSH-Kitaev模型在狄拉克自旋液体与非公度/公度价键固相（与Z₂拓扑序共存）之间存在一个分数量子临界点。低能场论揭示了这一分数量子临界点具有涌现的N=2时空超对称性，并讨论了其在热输运和粘度中的普适实验特征。

item.title: 第一性原理识别GaAs中光学高效的铒中心
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Khang Hoang
本文利用第一性原理混合泛函缺陷计算，系统研究了GaAs中与铒（Er）相关的缺陷，包括由Er与本征点缺陷或氧杂质组成的复合体。研究发现这些缺陷的电子结构和能量学对n型和p型掺杂表现出不对称性，并倾向于捕获电子。基于计算出的缺陷能级、形成能和非辐射载流子捕获系数，作者识别出在宿主光激发或少数载流子注入下，作为Er³⁺激发的高效陷阱辅助非辐射复合中心的Er相关缺陷。研究结果为理解特定缺陷中心（Er与两个氧原子耦合）的高效率以及n型和p型掺杂的影响提供了深入见解。

item.title: 三角形马约拉纳-哈伯德梯形格中的对称保护拓扑相
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Will Holdhusen, Alberto Nocera, Jian-Xin Zhu, Armin Rahmani
本文揭示了在三角形晶格梯形结构上相互作用的马约拉纳费米子模型中存在一系列对称保护拓扑相。其中包括一种对称保护拓扑序与伴随绝热演化对称性的自发对称破缺共存的相。研究结果表明，马约拉纳模式之间的相互作用可以产生非平凡的对称分数化序。这些相可能在拓扑超导体表面涡旋束缚的马约拉纳零模阵列中实现。

item.title: Efficient Quantum Circuits for Coherent Conversion Between General First- and Second-Quantized Many-Body Representations
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Jack S. Baker, Gaurav Saxena, Thi Ha Kyaw
arXiv:2606.25029v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Quantum simulation at fixed particle number admits two equivalent descriptions, a first-quantized (particle) representation and a second-quantized (occupation-number) representation. Their quantum resource costs differ sharply across computational tasks, so the ability to convert coherently between them is valuable. We construct an explicit unitary Q, with inverse Q^dagger, that maps a first-quantized state to its fixed-N occupation-number form while diagnosing the input's particle-exchange symmetry. The conversion is therefore symmetry-agnostic at the input yet fully resolved at the output, and it applies uniformly to bosonic, fermionic, and parastatistical sectors. At its foundation lies a structural identification that we place at the cen…

item.title: Discovery of connectivity-trainability trade-off of IQP Circuits for Hamiltonian Optimization
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Quoc Chuong Nguyen
arXiv:2606.24264v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: Instantaneous Quantum Polynomial-time (IQP) circuits are promising candidates for near-term quantum advantage due to the conjectured classical hardness of their sampling task. However, their capabilities for optimization remain largely unexplored. We present a systematic investigation of the performance and trainability of IQP circuits for Hamiltonian optimization. Our results reveal a trade-off between optimization performance and circuit connectivity, demonstrating that the circuit structure plays a key role in determining the ability of IQP circuits to reach low-energy states.

item.title: Spin modulations in the Rashba-Hubbard chain -- a tensor network study
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Jozef Genzor, Roman Krčmár, Andrej Gendiar, Chia-Min Chung, Denis Kochan
arXiv:2606.26014v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Uniform spin-orbit coupling in an open single-band Hubbard chain is an exactly removable (SU(2)) gauge field at the Hamiltonian level, but not at the level of laboratory-frame spin correlations. We study this separation using density matrix renormalization group calculations for the repulsive one-dimensional Rashba-Hubbard chain. For open boundary conditions, a site-dependent spin rotation maps the model with hopping (t) and Rashba spin-orbit strength (łambda) onto the ordinary Hubbard chain with renormalized hopping (t_łambda=sqrtt²⁺łambda²). Consequently, charge and energy diagnostics are affected only through the bandwidth renormalization, which is quadratic in weak (łambda/t). Spin correlations, however, respond already at linear order b…

item.title: Diagnosing energy gap in quantum spin liquids via polarization amplitude
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Takayuki Yokoyama, Yasuhiro Tada
arXiv:2602.12990v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Identifying whether a many-body ground state is gapped or gapless is a fundamental yet challenging problem, especially in quantum spin liquids. In this work, we develop a gap-diagnostic scheme based on the polarization amplitude defined via a twist operator, evaluated within the infinite density-matrix renormalization group (iDMRG) framework. As a benchmark, analysis of the spin-1/2 XXZ chain demonstrates that the polarization amplitude clearly distinguishes the gapless Tomonaga-Luttinger liquid from the gapped Néel phase. We then extend this framework to infinite cylinders of the spin-1/2 XY-J_ḩi model on the square lattice. We find that the polarization amplitude sharply detects the transition between the gapless XY phase and the gappe…

item.title: Lyapunov controlled counterdiabatic quantum optimization
item.description:
📚 Quantum Science and Technology
👤 Pranav Chandarana, Koushik Paul, Kasturi Ranjan Swain, Xi Chen and Adolfo del Campo
We introduce a quantum algorithm that integrates counterdiabatic (CD) protocols with quantum Lyapunov control (QLC) to address combinatorial optimization problems. This approach offers versatility, allowing implementation as either a digital-analog or purely digital algorithm based on selected control strategies. By examining spin-glass Hamiltonians, we illustrate how the algorithm can explore alternative paths to enhance solution outcomes compared to conventional CD techniques. This method reduces dependence on extensive higher-order CD terms and on classical optimization techniques, making it more suitable for existing quantum computing platforms. The combination of digital compression via CD protocols and the adaptable nature of QLC methods positions this approach as a promising candid…

item.title: Variants of the Quantum Phase Operator for the Harmonic Oscillator
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Bogdan D. Djordjevic, Nikolay A. Ivanov
arXiv:2606.24913v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce and study quantum phase operators associated with the Quantum Harmonic Oscillator (QHO). We show that these operators are trace-class perturbations of the Susskind-Glogower operators and examine their mathematical and physical properties. The construction is motivated by the physically relevant two-phase case.

item.title: Constraint-Aware Quantum Optimization of Defect Configurations in Doped ZrO2: XY-Mixer QAOA and Grover Adaptive Search
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Huajing Song
arXiv:2606.24922v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Quantum optimization offers a route to searching the large defect-configuration spaces that arise in materials design. We develop an end-to-end, constraint-aware quantum optimization workflow for composition-defect search in a doped ZrO2 thermal-barrier-coating (TBC) material system, using a MACE-MPA-0 energy dataset to fit a 24-variable QUBO over 8 cation-occupation and 16 oxygen-vacancy variables with exactly two rare-earth substitutions and one oxygen vacancy, yielding 448 feasible configurations. The QUBO surrogate reproduces the MACE energies with held-out R2 = 0.997 (full-data R2 = 0.999, RMSE = 17 meV). We validate two complementary quantum pathways against exact enumeration: a constraint-preserving XY-mixer QAOA that confines samplin…

item.title: Recursive QLSTM with Dynamic Variational Quantum Circuit Adaptation
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Samuel Yen-Chi Chen, Yifeng Peng, Jiun-Cheng Jiang, Chun-Hua Lin, Kuo-Chung Peng, Junghoon Justin Park, Huan-Hsin Tseng, Hsin-Yi Lin, Kuan-Cheng Chen, Chen-Yu Liu, Shinjae Yoo
arXiv:2606.24932v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advances in quantum computing and machine learning have motivated the development of quantum models for sequential data processing. In this paper, we propose a Recursive Quantum Long Short-Term Memory model, or Recursive QLSTM, which extends QLSTM through metacore-based recursive constructions. We numerically test the model under different input sequence lengths, metacore designs, and recursive rules, and identify the best-performing architecture among these variants. For this selected model, we further provide theoretical arguments explaining why its recursive structure improves temporal information propagation and enhances learning performance. Our results suggest that Recursive QLSTM offers a flexible and effective framework for qu…

item.title: Self-Modulating Quantum Fast-Weight Programmers for Efficient Adaptive Sequential Learning
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Samuel Yen-Chi Chen, Yifeng Peng, Kuo-Chung Peng, Jiun-Cheng Jiang, Chun-Hua Lin, Junghoon Justin Park, Huan-Hsin Tseng, Hsin-Yi Lin, Kuan-Cheng Chen, Chen-Yu Liu, Shinjae Yoo
arXiv:2606.24933v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advances in quantum machine learning have motivated efficient models for sequential data processing. In this paper, we propose Self-Modulating Quantum Fast Weight Programmers, or Self-Modulating QFWP, which extends Quantum Fast Weight Programmers by introducing adaptive modulation over both newly generated fast-weight updates and historical fast-weight memory. Numerical results show that the proposed mechanism improves convergence stability and prediction performance across varying model settings, including different numbers of qubits and input sequence lengths. We further provide theoretical arguments explaining how self-modulation balances new information injection with memory retention, thereby enhancing temporal information propag…

item.title: A Contactless Heat Engine Driven by Nonreciprocal Fluctuation-Induced Torques
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Dhruv Shah, Kiryl Asheichyk, David Gelbwaser-Klimovsky, Noah Graham, Mehran Kardar, Matthias Krüger
arXiv:2606.25053v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We describe a contactless heat engine in which quantum and thermal electromagnetic fluctuations act as the working medium. The setup consists of two concentric cylinders held at different temperatures. The inner cylinder stably levitates within the outer one due to repulsive nonequilibrium Casimir forces. The chirality of the setup is broken by using nonreciprocal dielectric materials, akin to application of a magnetic field along the common cylinder axis. Using Rytov fluctuational electrodynamics, we show that heat transfer and torque can be expressed in terms of an angular-momentum-resolved heat flux density, Φₙ₍ømega): each exchanged photon carries energy hbar ømega and angular momentum hbar n. In reciprocal media contributions from modes…

item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Pranjal Agarwal, Nada Ali, Mark Hillery
arXiv:2606.25100v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A discrete outcome quantum sensor network is one in which we are only interested in which detectors are activated. This can be studied in either the strong or weak interaction regime. If the detectors interact strongly with the environment, it is possible to definitely find which ones were activated. If the interaction is weaker, there is a possibility of making an error, and the object is to minimize the probability of this happening. Here we will be interested in this weaker interaction regime. We will also assume that only certain patterns of detectors will be activated, different patterns being translated versions of a fundamental one. Our object will be to find which pattern has been activated. We will look at both one and two-dimension…

item.title: Feasibility-driven QAOA with penalty scheduling
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Francesco Ferrari, Matteo Vandelli, Daniele Dragoni
arXiv:2606.25117v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Most available quantum algorithms address constrained optimization problems by treating constraints as soft penalty terms within a QUBO formulation. This approach requires careful adjustment of the penalty coefficients, which scales poorly with the number of constraints and lacks a proper strategy to balance feasibility and solution quality. In this work, we introduce two extensions of standard linear-ramp QAOA (lr-QAOA) tailored to problems with multiple heterogeneous constraints. We first construct Łambda-lr-QAOA, in which each penalty term is assigned its own linear-ramp schedule, promoting penalty weights from external hyperparameters to internal variational parameters of QAOA, similarly to the objective and mixer parameters. By optimizi…

item.title: Self-testing Quantum Supermaps
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Victor Barizien, Cyril Branciard, Alastair A. Abbott, Jean-Daniel Bancal, Pavel Sekatski
arXiv:2606.25124v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: By certifying quantum operations from measurement statistics directly, without any assumption on the internal workings of the devices involved, self-testing enables a uniquely reliable identification of quantum objects. While such device-independent characterization has been shown to be possible for states, measurements and channels, it has so far not been extended to quantum supermaps -- operations that act on quantum channels themselves and can combine them in either a well-defined causal order or also, remarkably, in an indefinite causal order. Here we show that quantum supermaps can be identified device-independently. Specifically, we obtain two levels of certification, depending on the network structure of the experiment: when each slot…

item.title: Closed Quantum Boltzmann Bridges: Coherent Revivals, Hidden Microstates, and the Emergence of Classical Two-Time Entropy Conditioning
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Sina Kazemian, Ghazal Farhani, Younes Javanmard
arXiv:2606.25260v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The classical Boltzmann Bridge describes entropy histories conditioned on both an initial low-entropy macrostate and a later macrostate. Unlike the usual past-only formulation of the thermodynamic arrow, this two-time conditioning can produce entropy profiles that rise above the final entropy and then decrease toward the imposed endpoint. In this work, we formulate closed quantum analogues of the Boltzmann Bridge using macro-subspace projectors, unitary time evolution, and Boltzmann entropy defined by the dimension of coarse-grained macroscopic sectors. We first study a minimal coherent chamber-qubit model, in which each particle has only a two-state chamber degree of freedom. Although this model is the most direct quantization of the classi…

item.title: A Mean-Field Lindblad Master Equation Framework for Interaction-Driven Decoherence in Solid-State Qubit Ensembles
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Dhiman Nandi, Sanghamitra Neogi
arXiv:2606.25261v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-qubit systems are essential for scalable quantum technologies, but their performance is often limited by decoherence from qubit--qubit interactions and environmental noise. Although environmental decoherence in single-qubit systems and gate fidelity in multi-qubit systems have been widely studied, a predictive framework connecting qubit interactions, concentration, spatial distribution, and bath occupation to relaxation and decoherence times remains lacking. Here, we develop a multi-qubit mean-field Lindblad master equation (MQMF-LME) framework for the population and coherence dynamics of a solid-state qubit in an interacting multi-qubit environment. The framework treats one qubit as the system of interest and the surrounding qubits as…

item.title: Routing Codes: High-Rate Quantum LDPC Codes with Short, Parallel Non-Local Connectivity
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Jiaxuan Zhang, Zhao-Yun Chen, Peng Duan, Jia-Ning Li, Tian-Hao Wei, Qing-Yang Hou, Wei-Cheng Kong, Yu-Chun Wu, Guo-Ping Guo
arXiv:2606.25330v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Quantum low-density parity-check (qLDPC) codes are promising candidates for realizing large-scale fault-tolerant quantum computing. Although many codes with favorable theoretical parameters have been developed, their practical adoption must take hardware implementability into account. For mainstream quantum platforms such as superconductors and neutral atoms, the connectivity, the length of non-local couplings, and the complexity of wiring or atom rearrangement are key factors that dictate the difficulty of hardware realization. Here, we propose a new family of qLDPC codes, termed routing codes. Within this family, we find explicit instances whose encoding rates are comparable to those of bivariate bicycle (BB) codes, while systematically re…

item.title: Preparing two-mode magnonic Schrödinger cat states in a cavity-magnon-qubit system
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Gen Li, Gang Liu, Rong-Can Yang, Jie Li
arXiv:2606.25511v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The cavity-magnon-qubit system has recently been demonstrated as a new platform for preparing macroscopic quantum states in magnonic systems. Here, we propose to prepare a two-mode magnonic cat state, which is also a non-Gaussian entangled state, based on this practical system involving two yttrium-iron-garnet (YIG) spheres and a superconducting qubit coupled to a common microwave cavity. By adiabatically eliminating the cavity and resonantly driving the qubit, an effective magnon-qubit conditional-displacement interaction is achieved. Further working in the magnon-magnon strong-coupling regime and considering two identical magnon frequencies and coupling strengths to the cavity, two hybridized magnon modes are formed, of which the bright mo…

item.title: The Cost of Removing Tunability in Quantum Data Re-Uploading
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Anthony Yuezhang Liu, Lirandë Pira
arXiv:2606.25598v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Fixed encoding data re-uploading quantum circuits provide a striking example of universality emerging from a highly constrained architecture. However, universality alone is insufficient for assessing the theoretical and practical value of fixed and tunable upload circuits. The resource cost of removing tunability remains poorly understood. In this work, we establish quantitative depth-error scaling for approximating tunable upload circuits with fixed upload circuits. We show that a tunable upload circuit can be approximated by a fixed upload circuit using depth ( D = O_σ!łeft[(łog(1/ǎrepsilon))^σi̊ght] ) for every (σ>1), with a target dependent constant overhead, thereby improving the previously known polynomial dependence on (1/ǎrepsilon) w…

item.title: Exact Leg-Cut Influence Functional and Emergence of Gaussian Entanglement Theory in a Statistical-Dressing Ladder Model
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Babatunde Moses Ayeni
arXiv:2606.25669v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The emergence of Gaussian effective field theories in low-dimensional quantum systems is traditionally understood through top-down frameworks such as bosonization and Luttinger-liquid theory. However, these approaches typically focus on the long-wavelength degrees of freedom in ways that do not directly track how non-Gaussian lattice-scale correlations are progressively discarded under coarse-graining. In this work, we present an exact lattice formulation from which this phenomenon emerges analytically. We analyze a two-leg hard-core ladder under a leg bipartition, where non-local statistical strings cross the entanglement cut. We construct an exact lattice influence-functional representation showing that the reduced state factorizes strictl…

item.title: Quantum steering in networks: Measurement-device-independent detection, continuous variables, and practical Gaussian schemes
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Shao-Hua Hu, Chung-Yun Hsieh, Huan-Yu Ku, Ray-Kuang Lee, Paolo Abiuso
arXiv:2606.25690v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We consider quantum steering certification in multipartite networks, with a focus on minimal trust scenarios: all-except-one parties are untrusted and treated device-independently. We show that it is always possible to lift steering certification to the measurement-device-independent regime, in which even the (last) trusted party can treat their local hardware as a black-box, except for a set of fiduciary quantum states used as the inputs to the experiment. This holds both for finite-dimensional systems as well as for bosonic continuous-variable systems, for which we provide a full characterization in the bipartite case. Additionally, we introduce measurement-device-independent network steering protocols based entirely on Gaussian operations…

item.title: A Short Note on the Generators of Controlled Quantum Gates
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Richard M. Milbradt, Christian B. Mendl
arXiv:2606.25789v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present the analytical generators for arbitrary multi-qubit controlled gates. Closed forms for the generating Hamiltonians are given for gates with both multiple control and target qubits, as well as for arbitrary control conditions. This allows us to go beyond gate-based simulations of quantum circuits and incorporate decoherence and other noise in simulations of quantum computers. We exemplify this by simulating the impact of a harmonic oscillator interacting with two qubits during the application of a controlled NOT gate.

item.title: Benchmarking Dark Matter Search using a Parity-Check Protocol with Machine-Learning Optimized Pulses
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yu-Han Chang, Ilya Moskalenko, Marko Kuzmanović, Ognjen Stanisavljević, Isak Björkman, David Díez-Ibáñez, Yikun Gu, Akash V. Dixit, Igor G. Irastorza, Gheorghe Sorin Paraoanu
arXiv:2606.25795v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We report on an improved microwave detection protocol for dark matter candidates such as the axion and the dark photon. We employ a superconducting transmon qubit dispersively coupled to a double-cavity system, enabling quantum non-demolition measurements of the photon occupation in a relatively short-lived storage cavity. To reduce the experimental cycle time and enhance sensitivity for axion and dark-photon searches, we operate this detector in a regime of increased qubit-cavity coupling, resulting in Stark shifts of 4.6 MHz. In this regime, conventional control pulses suffer from strong frequency-detuning sensitivity and photon-number-dependent errors. We address this limitation by implementing frequency-detuning-robust π/2 pulses (obtain…

item.title: A Candidate Framework for Free-Space Quantum Key Distribution based on Geometrical-Configuration Modulation
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yu-Ming Bai, Yu-Xuan Liu, Ming-Han Ding, Jun-Lin Li
arXiv:2606.25807v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper proposes a candidate framework for free-space quantum key distribution (QKD) based on geometrical-configuration modulation (GM). In the minimal implementation considered here, Alice coherently splits a single photon emitted from one source into two spatial output modes with a tunable separation, and uses the source separation R as the GM variable that defines the prepared single-photon spatial superposition state. Bob records the single-photon detection coordinate in the far field or Fourier plane, providing the correlated data used for soft-input information reconciliation. Based on this physical mechanism, we first establish an R-x protocol model in which the source separation R and the single-photon detection coordinate x are r…

item.title: Evolving Quantum Error-Correcting Encodings for Molecular Simulation
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Kenny Heitritter, James Brown, Tarini Hardikar
arXiv:2606.25870v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Useful quantum algorithms require many coupled discrete design choices. We study LLM-driven evolutionary program synthesis -- a language model edits a program, an external verifier scores the result, and high-scoring programs are retained and re-mutated -- as a tool for quantum-computing research. As a case study, we apply this loop to the Generalized Superfast Encoding (GSE), a fermion-to-qubit encoding whose prior molecular constructions reach code distance 3. The search discovered interpretable constructor programs whose codes have emphexact distance 5 on the molecular instances tested, and distance 6 on one 20-mode instance, under strict stabilizer-coset semantics. To our knowledge these are the first GSE/superfast encodings beyond dista…

item.title: Estimating Fidelity to a Reference Quantum State
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Qisheng Wang
arXiv:2606.26034v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We consider the problem of estimating the fidelity of an unknown quantum state to a known reference state to within additive error ǎrepsilon. We show that the sample complexity is O(r²/ǎrepsilon²⁾ with optimal ǎrepsilon-dependence when the reference state is of rank r, improving the previous best O(r²łog²⁽¹/ǎrepsilon)/ǎrepsilon⁴⁾ due to Utsumi, Nakata, Wang, and Takagi (QIP 2026). We also provide a lower bound of Ømega(r/ǎrepsilon²⁾, improving the previous best Ømega(r/ǎrepsilon+1/ǎrepsilon²⁾, with implications to quantum query complexity. Moreover, we further consider the case where the unknown state is of rank at most r while the reference state can be arbitrary, for which the sample complexity is shown to be O(r²/ǎrepsilon⁴⁾. As an applic…

item.title: Analytic Approach to Quantum Control Using Quantum Signal Processing
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Aishwarya Majumdar, John M. Martyn, Yuan Liu, Nathan Wiebe
arXiv:2606.26085v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Realizing coherent quantum computation requires precise and robust manipulation of quantum systems through quantum control protocols. Most quantum control techniques rely on heuristic methods for designing the driving pulses that steer the system towards a target state. Such methods are often based on brute-force optimization and offer limited understanding of the solution landscape. In contrast, quantum algorithms offer a rich body of analytical methods with rigorous error guarantees for implementing unitary and non-unitary transformations, which suggests a promising direction for developing new approaches to quantum control. Among various such algorithms, quantum signal processing (QSP) has emerged as a powerful framework for quantum algor…

item.title: Fast mixing of all-to-all quantum systems at high temperatures
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Thiago Bergamaschi
arXiv:2606.26090v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: It is shown that arbitrary quantum k-local Hamiltonians with bounded strength interactions admit a quantum Gibbs sampler [CKG23] with a system-size independent spectral gap, at sufficiently high temperatures. This generalizes the existing quantum fast-mixing results beyond the geometrically-local setting. As a consequence, such systems admit fully-polynomial time quantum approximation algorithms for partition functions and global expectation values.

item.title: From Meta Idea to Advanced Mathematical Discovery -- Human-AI Co-Discovery of Sign-Embedding Quantum Algorithms
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yanqiao Wang, Jin-Peng Liu, Peng Li, Yang Liu
arXiv:2606.24899v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: AI-assisted mathematics is often evaluated on solving predefined problems. In practice, however, many important advances begin earlier, when a vague research intuition is transformed into a concrete problem, a promising route, and a theorem family worth proving. This report studies that stage through a case study that led to sign-embedding quantum algorithms for matrix equations and matrix functions, foundational primitives in quantum linear algebra and operator-output quantum algorithms. The project began with a human-originated intuition that rational approximation is especially effective for jump-type functions such as the sign function, and might therefore serve as a design principle for quantum algorithms. Rather than merely assisting…

item.title: Halo-Independent Quantum Sensor Probes of Low-Velocity Dark Matter
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Muping Chen, Graciela B. Gelmini, Volodymyr Takhistov, Koichiro Yasuda
arXiv:2606.25129v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: We present a halo-independent framework for sub-GeV dark matter (DM) direct detection using quantum sensors with sub-eV energy thresholds. Such detectors enable access to low DM velocities and may be sensitive to departures from the Standard Halo Model that are challenging to probe with conventional direct DM detection experiments. The method expresses the DM scattering event rate in terms of a detector and particle model-dependent response function, and a universal halo function common to all experiments to be determined from data. This allows the local DM velocity distribution to be constrained. As representative implementations, we consider TES (Al) and MKID (TiN)-like sensors and show that their differing material responses probe compl…

item.title: Weak decay of the positronium ion
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Nishat Ul Sani, M. Jamil Aslam, Ishtiaq Ahmed
arXiv:2606.25433v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: The positronium ion (Ps⁻⁾, a coulombic three-body bound state of two electrons and a positron, predominantly decays via electron-positron annihilation into electromagnetic final states. While its radiative decay channels have been extensively studied, much less attention has been given to weak processes in this system. In this work, we investigate the rare decay Ps⁻ → e⁻ ν_μ barν_μ, obtained by replacing the photon in Ps⁻ → e⁻ γ with a virtual Z boson. Treating the three-body process as an effective two-body transition, Ps⁻ → e⁻ Z^*łeft(→ ν_μbarν_μi̊ght), we compute the decay rate by explicitly evaluating all spin configurations of the initial bound state and final particles. The result agrees with that obtained using the standard spin-sum…

item.title: Imposing Constraints on Driver Hamiltonians and Mixing Operators: From Theory to Practical Implementation
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Hannes Leipold, Federico M. Spedalieri, Stuart Hadfield, Eleanor Rieffel
arXiv:2407.01975v3 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Driver Hamiltonians and Mixing Operators that satisfy constraints is an important part of ansatz construction for many quantum algorithms. In this manuscript, we give general algebraic expressions for finding Hamiltonian terms and analogously unitary primitives, that satisfy constraint embeddings and use these to give complexity characterizations of the related problems. We prove that knowing if operators exist that enforce classical constraints is NP-Complete in the general case, but give algorithmic procedures with worse-case polynomial runtime to find any operators with a constant locality bound; a useful result since many constraints imposed admit local operators to enforce them in practice. We then give algorithmic procedures to tur…

item.title: Construction of new type of CNOT gate using cross-resonance pulse in the transmon-PPQ system
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Jeongsoo Kang, Younghun Kwon
arXiv:2501.15218v3 Announce Type: replace Abstract: The transmon, known for its fast operation time and the coherence time of tens of microseconds, is the most commonly used qubit for superconducting quantum processors. However, it is still necessary to enhance the coherence time and the gate fidelity of superconducting quantum processors for the practical implementation of fault-tolerant quantum computing. Meanwhile, a novel superconducting qubit, which has the ability to protect the Cooper-pair parity on the superconducting island, has been proposed. This new qubit shows better coherence performance than the transmon, but it does not yet have an efficient method for realizing a superconducting hybrid system that harnesses it. In this work, we show how to implement a new type of CNOT gat…

item.title: Resource-state Quantum RAM for Fast and Error-Correctable Queries
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Francesco Cesa, Hannes Bernien, Hannes Pichler
arXiv:2503.19172v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Quantum devices can process data in a fundamentally different way than classical computers. To leverage this potential, many algorithms require the aid of a quantum Random Access Memory (QRAM), i.e. a module capable of efficiently loading datasets onto the quantum processor. However, a realisation of this building block is still outstanding due to its formidable resource requirements, which become even more demanding in quantum error-correction schemes. Here we show that the challenge of implementing QRAM can be entirely reduced to a state-preparation problem: since such resource-state is independent on the memory, our approach allows one to prepare it offline, opening the door to new design strategies. As an example, we introduce a hera…

item.title: Quantum Optimal Control Using MAGICARP: Combining Pontryagin's Maximum Principle and Gradient Ascent
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Denis Janković, Jean-Gabriel Hartmann, Paul-Louis Etienney, Killian Lutz, Yannick Privat, Paul-Antoine Hervieux
arXiv:2505.21203v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We introduce the MAGICARP algorithm, a numerical optimization method for quantum optimal control problems that combines the structure provided by Pontryagin's Maximum Principle (PMP) and the robustness of gradient ascent techniques, such as GRAPE. MAGICARP is formulated as a "shooting technique", aiming to determine the appropriate initial adjoint momentum to realize a target quantum gate. This method naturally incorporates time and energy optimal constraints through a PMP-informed pulse structure. We demonstrate MAGICARP's effectiveness through illustrative numerical examples, comparing its performance to GRAPE and highlighting its advantages in specific scenarios.
